channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Bags of rubbish dumped yet again, 7th March
item.description:
Fly tipping, yet again
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Milk Street, Sundridge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter, 7th March
item.description:
Whenever our bins are emptied the road needs to be cleaned up! We used to have a regular “sweep “as being so close to the high street we get a large amount of rubbish.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Faversham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Human excrement on Aylwin Estate, 7th March
item.description:
Human excrement in car parking area outside 15-28 Aylwin Estate. Drug users use our estate as a toilet.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grange Walk, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish dumping, 7th March
item.description:
As above

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Faversham Road, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Vehicle parked in private bay without permission, 7th March
item.description:
This blue Ford van is parked in a private parking bay without the home owners permission, this has caused the bay owner to have to park elsewhere on the street away from home. It is also untaxed and without mot. This makes me believe it’s been dumped

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Iron Mill Lane, Crayford, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Sinkhole appearing near roundabout, 7th March
item.description:
Hole is growing day by day. Locations marked with arrow on photos

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Copers Cope Road, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rats in Cator Park near playground again., 7th March
item.description:
Three small rats (indicating that they are breeding) seen in vegetation next to the Beck waterway between the river and the children's playground close to the bridge.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Bailes Place, New Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Men gather and leave their litter behind in park, 7th March
item.description:
There have been improvements made to the communal garden on Ambergate Street in recent years, including new benches. Predictably these are a magnet for antisocial behaviour, noise and drug taking. One bench in particular is regularly used by local men to smoke, take drugs, eat, and generally cause a nuisance. Despite a litter bin being within two metres of the bench they leave their litter and cigarette butss all around the bench. I am sick of my taxes funding the clean up from such degenerate people but so be it. Please can the park receive a thorough litter pick?
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Ambergate Street, Elephant and Castle, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Litter strewn down road, 7th March
item.description:
Litter is everywhere

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Cranwich Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Stones and building debris, 7th March
item.description:
Recent roadworks have left stones/rubble and dirt on footpath and in the gutters on the roads. Both sides from the alleyway Upton 11 Rochester Avenue. Not washed away with recent rain
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12,14, Rochester Avenue, Widmore, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Street light no.4 outside 20 Howitt Road, 7th March
item.description:
Street light on all day.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Howitt Road, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: O'reilley street tp is sprayed, 7th March
item.description:
A shared use tp is sprayed on o'reilley street junction with willow walk zone gr
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): O'Reilly Street, Old Kent Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In between flats 36,37 and 38,39, communal back garden., 7th March
item.description:
This tree’s branches need pruning as the branches are almost touching the windows, when the tree is in full bloom the branches will eventually go though the windows. The tree is located in the back communal garden.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 29, Medburn Street, Somers Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pottery street parking sign is sprayed, 7th March
item.description:
A permit holders tp is sprayed on pottery street opp lp 01 zone g
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Pottery Street, Bermondsey, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Crease Park: Persistent issue with Dog walkers using Childrens Play Area on a daily basis, 7th March
item.description:
I live opposite Crease Park and there are daily occurrences of Dog walkers - 3 persistent daily users with large dogs, Alsatian and other large breeds – using the Children’s Play area for hours at a time. It is shameless, consistent use and a slow permanent incursion into what is a child’s area and marked, albeit poorly, as a dog free area incurring fines for dog use. My daughter was on the swing alone recently when one dog owner came up and starred at her trying to intimidate her into leaving so he could use the park for his dog. She outlasted his attempt. If these dogs can’t be off the lead in a public area they should not be off the lead in a designated kids area. It is happening all the time now. The children’s area is very run down but that shouldn’t be an incentive to turn it into dog, not child use. This has always been a minor issue in the decade I have been here but it has escalated hugely in the past year as it has become normalised.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Village Way, Beckenham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Opposite 27 Rochester Road - Detritus Left after Conway Roadworks, 7th March
item.description:
Large stone setts and broken cone left behind by Conway after they did re-surfacing.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 26, Rochester Road, Kentish Town, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti, 7th March
item.description:
Graffiti in green dragon court

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 58, Borough High Street, The Borough, Greater London

item.title: In West Street, next to the traffic lights, 7th March
item.description:

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, College Road, Plaistow, Greater London

item.title: Dog poo alongside 58 Aldeburgh Street, 7th March
item.description:
Dog poo at this location and other locations along Aldeburgh Street. This has been happening regularly for several weeks

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Aldeburgh Street, Greenwich
